title: tournesol obliqua
intro: Tournesol obliqua, also known as Marigold or Calendula, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of Tournesol obliqua lie in its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which make it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The health benefits associated with Tournesol obliqua include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections, making it a valuable treatment for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Tournesol obliqua include teas, tinctures, and creams, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
